title: Credential Stuffing — VPN Gateway Authentication Anomaly
raw_evidence: 2025-07-14T07:08:55Z — 1,240 failed authentication attempts against VPN gateway from 198.51.100.73 over 4 minutes.
2025-07-14T07:09:03Z — Attempts use valid username format (first.last@corp.example) — not random; suggests credential list from prior breach.
2025-07-14T07:11:44Z — 3 successful authentications during the spray window: accounts mwilliams, t.chen, r.patel.
2025-07-14T07:11:50Z — mwilliams session: immediate download of IT asset inventory spreadsheet (unusual — no prior VPN file access for this account).
2025-07-14T07:12:01Z — t.chen session: MFA challenge issued and passed within 4 seconds (unusually fast; possible MFA fatigue / push bombing).
2025-07-14T07:12:19Z — r.patel session: connection dropped after 8 seconds — no file access.
2025-07-14T07:13:00Z — Source IP 198.51.100.73 geolocated to residential proxy service — not a known corporate location.

context.asset_criticality_destination: HIGH — external VPN gateway, entry point to internal network
